title: Corpus annotation entry
description: Per-declaration annotation YAML for leray-hopf-notes. One file per declaration at corpus/<module-path>/<decl-name>.yaml.

name.description: Fully-qualified Lean declaration name (matches extracted/names-fallback.json or decls.json).

file.description: Lean source file path from extracted/decls.json. Optional for unique names; required when decls.json contains multiple declarations with this display name.

tier.description: Annotation depth. 'full' = statement_ja + proof_ja + gap; 'gloss' = 1-3 line role summary only.

statement_ja.description: Japanese translation of the mathematical statement. Required for both tiers.

proof_ja.description: Japanese proof narrative (textbook style). Required when tier=full AND the declaration is theorem-like (theorem/lemma). Optional for def/structure/instance/abbrev. Omit (or leave null) for gloss tier.

gap.description: Formalization gap assessment: how much more verbose is the Lean proof vs. the natural-language proof. Orthogonal to `proof_status`: `gap` measures formalization overhead on an already-proved statement, it is not a proof-completion signal — a `large` gap can be `proof_status: verified` and a `none`/`mild` gap can still be `contains-sorry` etc. See `proof_status`'s description for the actual completion axis.

level.description: none = Lean proof closely follows natural language; mild = minor extra technicalities; large = significant formalization overhead.

note.description: Explanation of what inflates the Lean proof. Required when level=large.

chapter.description: Chapter assignment. Must be one of the chapter ids defined in docs/schemas/chapters.yaml.

tags.description: Optional free-form tags for cross-cutting concerns (e.g. ['rellich', 'frechet-kolmogorov']).

proof_status.description: Machine-checked proof completion status, independent of `tier` (annotation depth) AND independent of `gap` (formalization-overhead size, not a completion signal). Absence means 'verified': no known literal `sorry`, no known false/over-general statement, not a historical scaffold or retired declaration. 'verified' may also be written explicitly. 'contains-sorry' = the proof body has a literal `sorry` (directly or via a sorry-dependent private helper). 'scaffold' = an early, intentionally vacuous/placeholder declaration kept for historical record, not part of the current proof route. 'retired' = superseded or removed from the release surface, kept in the corpus for provenance only. 'invalid-statement' = the statement as typed is known to be false or over-general (quarantined pending a source fix); statement_ja/proof_ja or gap.note must explain why. Historical axiom-to-theorem status changes (e.g. 'this was an axiom, proved in issue #NN') belong in `provenance`, not in this field, which reports only the current status.

provenance.description: Development-process history (issue/PR numbers, review rounds, axiom-to-theorem flips, module reorganizations, AI-assisted-review notes). Kept for the historical record but rendered in a collapsed/hidden panel, separate from the default scholarly view (statement_ja/proof_ja/gap.note), per notes#69: default declaration prose should read as mathematics, not issue-tracker archaeology.

references.description: Bibliographic citations backing this declaration's statement, proof, or naming/definition choices. Each `id` must match a `### `id`` heading in docs/bibliography.md (scripts/validate.py cross-checks this).

id.description: Citation key matching a docs/bibliography.md entry (e.g. 'temam2001').

locator.description: Optional pinpoint within the work: chapter/section/theorem/page (e.g. 'III.3', '§3.2', 'Thm 2, p. 302').
